questions.question: Which key holds the episode's content in the context passed to the attribute extraction prompt?
questions.answer: In `resolve_extracted_edge` (graphiti_core/utils/maintenance/edge_operations.py), the attribute‐extraction context uses the key `"episode_content"` to hold the episode’s content.
facts: resolve_extracted_edge is defined in graphiti_core/utils/maintenance/edge_operations.py.
The attribute-extraction context in resolve_extracted_edge uses the key "episode_content".
The key "episode_content" holds the episode’s content.

questions.question: Which two modules' Cypher queries were updated to include the name_embedding field in their return statements?
questions.answer:
The Cypher queries in

• graphiti_core/nodes.py (EntityNode.get_by_uuid)  
• graphiti_core/search/search_utils.py (get_mentioned_nodes)  

were both updated to add `n.name_embedding` to their RETURN clauses.
facts: The file graphiti_core/nodes.py contains a Cypher query in the function EntityNode.get_by_uuid.
The Cypher query in graphiti_core/nodes.py (EntityNode.get_by_uuid) was updated to add n.name_embedding to its RETURN clause.
The file graphiti_core/search/search_utils.py contains a Cypher query in the function get_mentioned_nodes.
The Cypher query in graphiti_core/search/search_utils.py (get_mentioned_nodes) was updated to add n.name_embedding to its RETURN clause.

questions.question: In the search handler, how is the query’s max_facts value applied to limit the number of graph search results?
questions.answer: In server/graph_service/routers/retrieve.py’s search handler, `query.max_facts` is passed straight into the `num_results` parameter of `graphiti.search(...)`. That tells the graph search implementation (in ZepGraphitiDep) to return at most that many edges, effectively capping your results to `max_facts`.
facts: In server/graph_service/routers/retrieve.py’s search handler, query.max_facts is passed directly into the num_results parameter of graphiti.search(...).
graphiti.search(...) uses a graph search implementation in ZepGraphitiDep.
The graph search implementation in ZepGraphitiDep returns at most the number of edges specified by its num_results parameter.
Passing query.max_facts into the num_results parameter effectively caps the search results to query.max_facts edges.

questions.question: What does the search function return when given an empty or whitespace-only query?
questions.answer:
In graphiti_core/search/search.py’s search(), if query.strip() == "" it immediately returns a SearchResults with all empty lists:

• edges = []  
• nodes = []  
• episodes = []  
• communities = []
facts: graphiti_core/search/search.py defines a function named search().
search() checks if query.strip() == "".
If query.strip() == "", search() immediately returns a SearchResults object.
The returned SearchResults object has an attribute edges equal to an empty list.
The returned SearchResults object has an attribute nodes equal to an empty list.
The returned SearchResults object has an attribute episodes equal to an empty list.
The returned SearchResults object has an attribute communities equal to an empty list.

questions.question: What field names are used to specify the model in the LLM client config versus the embedder client config?
questions.answer:
In GraphitiLLMConfig.create_client you pass your model in LLMConfig.model (and optionally small_model), e.g.:

• LLMConfig(api_key=…, model=self.model, small_model=self.small_model, …)

In GraphitiEmbedderConfig.create_client you use:

• AzureOpenAIEmbedderClient(…, model=self.model)  
• OpenAIEmbedderConfig(api_key=…, embedding_model=self.model)

So the OpenAIEmbedderConfig field is named embedding_model, whereas LLMConfig uses model.
facts: In GraphitiLLMConfig.create_client, the model is passed via the LLMConfig field model.
In GraphitiLLMConfig.create_client, a small model can be passed via the LLMConfig field small_model.
In GraphitiEmbedderConfig.create_client, AzureOpenAIEmbedderClient is used with model=self.model.
In GraphitiEmbedderConfig.create_client, OpenAIEmbedderConfig is used with embedding_model=self.model.
The OpenAIEmbedderConfig field for the embedding model is named embedding_model.
The LLMConfig field for the model is named model.

questions.question: How does the code ensure additional edge attributes are included when saving and retrieving EntityEdge objects?
questions.answer:
When saving, EntityEdge.save (graphiti_core/edges.py) builds its payload dict and then does

• edge_data.update(self.attributes or {})

so any custom key/​value in the edge’s .attributes gets written as relationship properties.  

When loading (in all “get…” methods and the full-text/similarity/search utils) each Cypher query returns

• properties(r) AS attributes

and get_entity_edge_from_record(record) feeds record['attributes'] into the EntityEdge.attributes field (then pops out the reserved keys). This round-trips any extra attributes you added.
facts: EntityEdge.save is defined in graphiti_core/edges.py
EntityEdge.save builds its payload dictionary
EntityEdge.save calls edge_data.update(self.attributes or {})
Any custom key/value in an edge’s attributes is written as relationship properties when saving
In the get methods and the full-text, similarity, and search utilities, each Cypher query returns properties(r) AS attributes
get_entity_edge_from_record(record) assigns record['attributes'] to EntityEdge.attributes
get_entity_edge_from_record(record) removes reserved keys from the attributes
Extra attributes added to an edge are preserved through save and load operations

questions.question: How is the effective group_id determined for add_memory when no group_id argument is provided?
questions.answer:
In add_memory (mcp_server/graphiti_mcp_server.py), you’ll see:

•  
effective_group_id = group_id if group_id is not None else config.group_id  
•  
group_id_str = str(effective_group_id) if effective_group_id is not None else ''  

So when you don’t pass a group_id, it falls back to the global config.group_id (as set by GraphitiConfig.from_cli_and_env, defaulting to “default” if no CLI/env override), then casts it to a string.
facts: In the add_memory function in mcp_server/graphiti_mcp_server.py, effective_group_id is assigned to group_id if group_id is not None, otherwise to config.group_id.

In the add_memory function in mcp_server/graphiti_mcp_server.py, group_id_str is assigned to the string conversion of effective_group_id if effective_group_id is not None, otherwise to an empty string.
If no group_id is passed to add_memory, effective_group_id falls back to config.group_id.
config.group_id is set by GraphitiConfig.from_cli_and_env.
config.group_id defaults to “default” if there is no CLI or environment variable override.

questions.question: How is the small model name selected and applied to the LLM client configuration?
questions.answer:
In mcp_server/graphiti_mcp_server.py the small‐model name is chosen in GraphitiLLMConfig:

• from_env() reads os.environ['SMALL_MODEL_NAME'], defaults to SMALL_LLM_MODEL if unset or blank.  
• from_cli_and_env() will override config.small_model with args.small_model when you pass --small-model.  
• create_client() then passes self.small_model into the LLMConfig that gets handed off to OpenAIClient or AzureOpenAILLMClient.
facts: In mcp_server/graphiti_mcp_server.py, GraphitiLLMConfig chooses the small-model name.
The from_env() method reads the SMALL_MODEL_NAME environment variable.
If SMALL_MODEL_NAME is unset or blank, from_env() defaults to SMALL_LLM_MODEL.
The from_cli_and_env() method overrides config.small_model with args.small_model when --small-model is passed.
The create_client() method passes self.small_model into the LLMConfig.
The LLMConfig is handed off to OpenAIClient or AzureOpenAILLMClient.

questions.question: In the triplet insertion flow, how are deduplication tasks for entities and facts orchestrated before persisting the data?
questions.answer:
Entity and fact de-duplication in the triplet flow is entirely handled before the bulk write in Graphiti.add_triplet (graphiti_core/graphiti.py):

1. resolve_extracted_nodes (graphiti_core/utils/maintenance/node_operations.py)  
   – runs hybrid search + the dedupe_nodes.nodes LLM prompt to map each extracted EntityNode to an existing or new node  
   – returns resolved_nodes and a uuid_map for edge pointer resolution  

2. resolve_edge_pointers uses that uuid_map to rewire the new edge to the deduped nodes  

3. Fetch relevant/invalidation candidates via get_relevant_edges and get_edge_invalidation_candidates  

4. resolve_extracted_edge (graphiti_core/utils/maintenance/edge_operations.py)  
   – calls the dedupe_edges.resolve_edge LLM prompt to identify duplicate facts, contradictions and fact types  
   – returns a single resolved_edge plus lists of invalidated and duplicate edges  

5. add_nodes_and_edges_bulk persists the deduped resolved_nodes and [resolved_edge + invalidated_edges]  

Both node and edge resolves use semaphore_gather to parallelize searches and LLM calls.
facts: Entity and fact de-duplication in the triplet flow takes place before the bulk write in Graphiti.add_triplet.
Graphiti.add_triplet is defined in graphiti_core/graphiti.py.
resolve_extracted_nodes is defined in graphiti_core/utils/maintenance/node_operations.py.
resolve_extracted_nodes runs hybrid search and the dedupe_nodes.nodes LLM prompt to map each extracted EntityNode to an existing or new node.
resolve_extracted_nodes returns resolved_nodes and a uuid_map for edge pointer resolution.
resolve_edge_pointers uses a uuid_map to rewire the new edge to the deduped nodes.
get_relevant_edges fetches relevant edge candidates.
get_edge_invalidation_candidates fetches invalidation edge candidates.
resolve_extracted_edge is defined in graphiti_core/utils/maintenance/edge_operations.py.
resolve_extracted_edge calls the dedupe_edges.resolve_edge LLM prompt to identify duplicate facts, contradictions, and fact types.
resolve_extracted_edge returns a single resolved_edge plus lists of invalidated and duplicate edges.
add_nodes_and_edges_bulk persists the deduped resolved_nodes and the resolved_edge plus invalidated edges.
Both node and edge resolves use semaphore_gather to parallelize searches and LLM calls.

facts: GeminiEmbedder.create_batch is defined in the file graphiti_core/embedder/gemini.py.
In GeminiEmbedder.create_batch, if embedding.values is empty in the batch response, the method raises a ValueError.
The ValueError raised by GeminiEmbedder.create_batch has the message "Empty embedding values returned".

questions.question: What is the sequence of operations from parsing a podcast message to generating deduplicated nodes and extracted edges?
questions.answer:
Here’s the end-to-end flow:

1. In examples/podcast/podcast_runner.py:main  
   – call parse_podcast_messages() → list of Message objects  
   – wrap each Message in a RawEpisode and invoke Graphiti.add_episode(…) or add_episode_bulk(…)

2. Inside Graphiti.add_episode / add_episode_bulk  
   a. create an EpisodicNode from your RawEpisode  
   b. **Extract raw EntityNode objects** (not shown in context)  
   c. call graphiti_core/utils/maintenance/node_operations.py::resolve_extracted_nodes()  
      – runs hybrid search over each extracted node  
      – builds LLM prompt via graphiti_core/prompts/dedupe_nodes.py::nodes()  
      – LLM returns entity_resolutions → you get resolved_nodes (and a uuid_map)  
   d. call graphiti_core/utils/maintenance/edge_operations.py::extract_edges()  
      – prepares context (episode.content, resolved_nodes, previous episodes, edge_type_map)  
      – prompts via graphiti_core/prompts/extract_edges.py::edge() (with reflexion loop)  
      – converts LLM output into EntityEdge objects with valid_at/invalid_at, source/target UUIDs  

3. finally, Graphiti persists the deduplicated nodes and extracted edges into Neo4j.
facts: The main function in examples/podcast/podcast_runner.py calls parse_podcast_messages().
parse_podcast_messages() returns a list of Message objects.
The main function wraps each Message object in a RawEpisode.
The main function invokes Graphiti.add_episode or Graphiti.add_episode_bulk with each RawEpisode.
Graphiti.add_episode and Graphiti.add_episode_bulk create an EpisodicNode from a RawEpisode.
Graphiti.add_episode and Graphiti.add_episode_bulk extract raw EntityNode objects.
Graphiti.add_episode and Graphiti.add_episode_bulk call resolve_extracted_nodes() from graphiti_core/utils/maintenance/node_operations.py.
resolve_extracted_nodes() runs a hybrid search over each extracted EntityNode.
resolve_extracted_nodes() builds an LLM prompt via graphiti_core/prompts/dedupe_nodes.py by calling nodes().
The LLM returns entity_resolutions, which yields resolved_nodes and a uuid_map.
Graphiti.add_episode and Graphiti.add_episode_bulk call extract_edges() from graphiti_core/utils/maintenance/edge_operations.py.
extract_edges() prepares a context consisting of episode.content, resolved_nodes, previous episodes, and edge_type_map.
extract_edges() prompts via graphiti_core/prompts/extract_edges.py by calling edge(), using a reflection loop.
extract_edges() converts LLM output into EntityEdge objects with valid_at and invalid_at timestamps and source and target UUIDs.
Graphiti persists the deduplicated nodes into Neo4j.
Graphiti persists the extracted edges into Neo4j.

questions.question: How does the server initialization select and configure LLM and embedding clients when Azure settings are provided versus default OpenAI?
questions.answer:
During startup (`initialize_graphiti` in mcp_server/graphiti_mcp_server.py) we call:

– config.llm.create_client()  
  • If azure_openai_endpoint is set:  
    – If azure_openai_use_managed_identity → use AsyncAzureOpenAI + managed identity token provider → AzureOpenAILLMClient  
    – Elif api_key → use AsyncAzureOpenAI + API key → AzureOpenAILLMClient  
    – Else → error  
  • Else (no Azure) → require OPENAI_API_KEY → return OpenAIClient  

– config.embedder.create_client()  
  • If azure_openai_endpoint is set: same managed-identity vs API-key logic → AzureOpenAIEmbedderClient  
  • Else if OPENAI_API_KEY → return OpenAIEmbedder  
  • Else → returns None  

Those clients are then passed into the Graphiti constructor.
facts: During startup, the initialize_graphiti function in mcp_server/graphiti_mcp_server.py is invoked.
initialize_graphiti calls config.llm.create_client.

If azure_openai_endpoint is set when calling config.llm.create_client and azure_openai_use_managed_identity is true, AsyncAzureOpenAI with managed identity token provider is used to create AzureOpenAILLMClient.
If azure_openai_endpoint is set when calling config.llm.create_client and api_key is provided, AsyncAzureOpenAI with API key is used to create AzureOpenAILLMClient.
If azure_openai_endpoint is set when calling config.llm.create_client and neither azure_openai_use_managed_identity nor api_key is provided, an error is triggered.
If azure_openai_endpoint is not set when calling config.llm.create_client, OPENAI_API_KEY is required and OpenAIClient is returned.
initialize_graphiti calls config.embedder.create_client.
If azure_openai_endpoint is set when calling config.embedder.create_client, managed-identity versus API-key logic is applied to create AzureOpenAIEmbedderClient.
If azure_openai_endpoint is not set when calling config.embedder.create_client and OPENAI_API_KEY is provided, OpenAIEmbedder is returned.
If azure_openai_endpoint is not set when calling config.embedder.create_client and OPENAI_API_KEY is not provided, None is returned.
The clients returned by config.llm.create_client and config.embedder.create_client are passed into the Graphiti constructor.
